FIN.text: -- true local candidate flags.  It does not promote the Clay Yang-Mills claim.
"SC1 uses the spectral resolution of T(a) to define log T(a) and H(a)."

GG1.text: -- Sprint128 WC1+SC1 compactness and log-generator closure.

OS1.text: "Sprint89 supplies the transfer positivity/reflection-positivity route, used here as 0 < T(a) <= I on the physical carrier."

SC1.text: -- Sprint128 WC1+SC1 compactness and log-generator closure.
-- * SC1 closes by the spectral theorem for the positive contraction
--   0 < T(a) <= I from Sprint89, giving the logarithmic functional calculus,
--   self-adjoint log generator, and physical-domain stability.

SY1.text: "The Borel functional calculus applies log on the spectrum of T(a), and H(a) = -a^-1 log T(a) is self-adjoint."

WC1.text: -- Sprint128 WC1+SC1 compactness and log-generator closure.
-- * WC1 closes by Banach-Alaoglu on the separable Hilbert space H_phys
--   constructed by CC1, with bounded energy balls and diagonal extraction.

string_assignments.ledgerName: Sprint128 WC1+SC1 compactness and log-generator closure

FIN.text: -- Dobrushin/polymer two-point input: the non-vacuum finite-lattice
-- comparison.  It does not promote the continuum Clay Yang-Mills theorem.
"This is a cutoff transfer-matrix comparison theorem; continuum Yang-Mills construction and Clay promotion remain outside this module."

SC3.text: -- Dobrushin/polymer two-point input: the non-vacuum finite-lattice
-- module promotes only the DASHI-native cutoff transfer lower-bound
"m_eff(a) is imported from WC3 Dobrushin/polymer two-point decay and not from the desired transfer or continuum mass gap."
"Sprint128 closes the DASHI-native SC3 transfer lower-bound comparison gap(T(a)) >= (1/4)*m_eff(a)*a from spectral calculus plus the WC3 polymer two-point Hamiltonian lower bound."
"This is a cutoff transfer-matrix comparison theorem; continuum Yang-Mills construction and Clay promotion remain outside this module."

string_assignments.closureStatementText: Sprint128 closes the DASHI-native SC3 transfer lower-bound comparison gap(T(a)) >= (1/4)*m_eff(a)*a from spectral calculus plus the WC3 polymer two-point Hamiltonian lower bound.
string_assignments.nonCircularityText: m_eff(a) is imported from WC3 Dobrushin/polymer two-point decay and not from the desired transfer or continuum mass gap.
string_assignments.nonClayBoundaryText: This is a cutoff transfer-matrix comparison theorem; continuum Yang-Mills construction and Clay promotion remain outside this module.
string_assignments.spectralCalculusFormulaText: 1 - exp(-a lambda) >= (a/2) * lambda on the Sprint125 small-a transfer window

FIN.text: -- coverage boundary is recorded, and finite k_G existence is closed here.
-- Clay promotion remains explicitly false: this is a native Sprint128
-- symmetry/group candidate closure, not a Clay submission promotion.

GG1.text: -- Sprint128 SY1+GG1 symmetry and compact-simple-group closure.
-- GG1 is promoted by the supplied compact simple group parameter table:
-- requested SU2/SU3/SUN/G2/E8 rows are concrete, the classification
-- symmetry/group candidate closure, not a Clay submission promotion.

OS1.text: -- Euclidean Schwinger covariance, OS/Wightman Poincare covariance, and the
"SO4-covariant Schwinger data are consumed by the OS/Wightman route to record DASHI-native Poincare covariance and positive-energy spectrum condition closure here."

SY1.text: -- Sprint128 SY1+GG1 symmetry and compact-simple-group closure.
-- checked Agda candidate-closure surface.  SY1 is promoted by the supplied
-- H3 x id_time lattice symmetry, a_k -> 0 anisotropy decay/tuning, SO4
-- Euclidean Schwinger covariance, OS/Wightman Poincare covariance, and the
-- positive-energy spectrum consumer are all recorded as closed here.
-- symmetry/group candidate closure, not a Clay submission promotion.

string_assignments.laneName: Sprint128 SY1+GG1 symmetry-restoration and compact-simple-group closure
